DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, JAN-10-2023 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART, AND CONTINUED IN PART. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.). MATTER IS CONTINUED FOR FURTHER HEARING TO JAN-31-2023 AT 9:00 A.M. ON THE DISCOVERY CALENDAR. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER IN WORD FORMAT, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM, AND THEN SUBMIT IT TO THE PRO TEM JUDGE AT RENNEPAR85@GMAIL.COM. Motion To Compel Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Continued To Jan-31-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. Vahe Shakhgeldyan, Esq. of BELORYAN & MANUKYAN LLP (Tel: 818-387-6428) for Plaintiff Edmond Tsarturyan;
Atty. Elizabeth Williams, Esq. of SKANE MILLS LLP (Tel: 415-431-4150) for Defendant.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF EDMOND TSARTURYAN's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable And Request For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF, EDMOND TSARTURYAN'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANT" OR "TSARTURYAN" IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOW:

(1) WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISO (HEREAFTER EITHER "CCSF" OR "DEFENDANT") WILL PRODUCE A PMK WITNESS FOR DEPOSITION ON THE SUBJECT MATTER SPECIFIED IN THE DEPOSITION NOTICE AS A PMK REGARDING ACCEPTED AND NON-ACCEPTED CITY STREETS. TOGATHER WITH PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN SEEKING THIS DEPOSITION SINCE MAY 2022. CCSF'S SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION SCHEDULED FOR JANUARTY 27, 2023 SPECIFICALLY RAISES THE ISSUE OF ACCEPTED/NON-ACCEPTED STREETS AND PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO SUCH DISCOVERY BEFORE A DECISION CAN BE MADE ON THE ISSUES RAISED IN THE S/J MOTION.
(2) THE COURT WILL CONTINUE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AS TO THE OTHER PMK DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS UNTIL JANUARY 31, 2023 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPARTMENT 301. PRIOR TO THAT DATE THE PARTIES SHOULD PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A COPY OF THE OBJECTIONS SERVED BY DEFENDANT TO THESE SUBPOENAS.
(3) THE COURT WILL CONTINUE UNTIL THE HEARING ON 1/31 ITS RULING ON THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.

Counsel for plaintiff/moving party is to prepare a proposed order in Word format, submit it to counsel for defendant for approval as to form, and then submit it to the pro tem judge at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane / R. Sahatjian; Not Reported (302/JPT)

MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS (PART 2 OF 2 FOR PURPOSES OF ENTRY OF TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-28-2022 Continued To Feb-27-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-30-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Haik Beloryan, Esq., BELORYAN & MANUKYAN LLP, (818) 387-6428, beloryanlaw@gmail.com for Plaintiff.

Motion is not contested. Plaintiff submits on the Court's tentative ruling.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's ("CCSF") motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant fails to maintain its burden of production per CCP 437c(p)(2) as it fails to demonstrate that the adduced release/limitation of liability clause is enforceable. CCSF's alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312.

Judge: Vedica Puri, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/VP)
